hero.subtitle: Send and receive webhooks through one platform. Outgoing delivery with retries and signatures. Incoming ingestion with validation and routing. You focus on building product.

quickWins.queueingDesc: Buffer and control event flow with a resilient queue that handles spikes, retries, and destination failures.
quickWins.replayDesc: Automatically surface failed events, inspect metadata, and replay them with confidence.
quickWins.devToolsDesc: Send test events, verify signatures, and replay deliveries — all from one unified interface.
quickWins.routingDesc: Filter and route events to one or many destinations with wildcard subscriptions and pattern matching.

trust.title: Scalable, secure,
trust.subtitle: Enterprise-grade security and reliability built into every layer of the platform.
trust.encryptionDesc: AES-GCM encryption at rest. TLS in transit. Secrets never stored in plaintext.
trust.hmacDesc: Every outgoing payload cryptographically signed. Incoming webhooks verified before forwarding.
trust.ssrfDesc: Built-in URL validation blocks internal network access and private IP ranges.
trust.rbacDesc: Owner, Developer, Viewer roles with granular permissions per organization.
trust.apiKeysDesc: Scoped API keys with project-level isolation. Rotate without downtime.
trust.auditDesc: Every action tracked — who did what, when, and from where.
trust.atLeastOnceDesc: Transactional outbox pattern ensures zero event loss even during failures.
trust.selfHostedDesc: Deploy on your own infrastructure. Full control over data and configuration.
trust.openSourceDesc: MIT licensed. Audit the code, contribute, and customize to your needs.

flowDiagram.title: See how
flowDiagram.subtitle: Hookflow sits between your application and external services — handling delivery, ingestion, retries, and observability in both directions.
flowDiagram.sendDesc: Your app fires events → Hookflow reliably delivers to all registered endpoints with retries, signatures, and monitoring.
flowDiagram.receiveDesc: Third-party services send webhooks → Hookflow ingests, validates, and routes them to your application.

capabilities.title: Built for teams that
capabilities.subtitle: Three pillars that make Hookflow the infrastructure layer your webhooks deserve.

retries.title: Smart retries with dead letter queue
retries.desc: Exponential backoff across 6 retry tiers (1m → 24h). Failed after all attempts? Automatically routed to DLQ for manual inspection and one-click replay.
retries.dlqDesc: Failed deliveries preserved for inspection, replay or bulk purge
retries.replayDesc: Re-deliver any event from any attempt — with dry-run preview

diagnostics.title: Error diagnostics & classification
diagnostics.desc: Every failed delivery is automatically classified into 11 error categories with severity levels, suggested fixes, and a visual retry timeline.
diagnostics.classifyDesc: Timeout, DNS, SSL, auth, rate-limit, 5xx — identified instantly
diagnostics.suggestDesc: Actionable guidance for each error type — no guesswork
diagnostics.timelineDesc: Visual dot-by-dot history of every delivery attempt

incoming.title: Receive, verify & forward — zero code
incoming.desc: Create ingress URLs for Stripe, GitHub, or any provider. Hookflow verifies HMAC signatures and forwards to your internal services with full retry support.
incoming.ingestDesc: Each source gets a dedicated URL with rate limiting
incoming.verifyDesc: Validate signatures from any provider before forwarding
incoming.forwardDesc: Route to one or more internal endpoints with retries

features.title: Everything you need for
features.subtitle: 12 battle-tested features. Zero compromises on reliability, security, or developer experience.
features.hmacDesc: Every payload cryptographically signed. Verify on your end.
features.dlqDesc: Failed deliveries preserved for inspection and one-click replay.
features.diagnosticsDesc: Auto-classify failures into 11 categories with suggested fixes.
features.incomingIngressDesc: Receive from Stripe, GitHub, Twilio — unique ingress URLs per source.
features.schemaRegistryDesc: JSON Schema contracts per event type. Version, diff, and promote.
features.wildcardRoutingDesc: Subscribe to order.* or ** for flexible event routing.
features.deterministicReplayDesc: Dry-run preview, idempotency keys, replay from any attempt.
features.analyticsDesc: Success rates, latency percentiles, endpoint health scores.
features.piiMaskingDesc: Redact sensitive fields in payloads and headers automatically.
features.auditLogDesc: Track every action — who did what, when, and from where.
features.rbacDesc: Owner, Developer, Viewer roles with granular permissions.

architecture.title: Battle-tested
architecture.subtitle: Every event is persisted atomically, published reliably, and delivered with full observability

observability.title: Full visibility into
observability.subtitle: See every attempt with full HTTP context — status codes, error messages, latency, and timestamps.
observability.timelineDesc: View all retry attempts with full HTTP context
observability.errorDetailsDesc: See exact error messages and response bodies
observability.perfMetricsDesc: Track latency percentiles and identify slow endpoints

devExperience.title: Developer-friendly API
devExperience.subtitle: Clean, predictable API. Send events in one request. We handle delivery, retries, and monitoring.
devExperience.accepted: Event accepted · ID: evt_abc123 · 3 deliveries queued

howItWorks.title: How it
howItWorks.subtitle: From first API call to full observability in under 5 minutes
howItWorks.step1Desc: One API call. That's it. We persist atomically and queue for delivery.
howItWorks.step2Desc: HMAC-signed payloads, exponential backoff, automatic retries across multiple intervals.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Full observability dashboard. See every attempt, replay failures, track success rates.

cta.title: Start sending and receiving webhooks reliably
cta.subtitle: Sign up, configure endpoints, and send or receive your first webhook in under 5 minutes.

socialProof.title: Built for
socialProof.subtitle: Architectural guarantees backed by battle-tested patterns — not marketing promises.
socialProof.quote1: We replaced 3 months of custom webhook code with Hookflow in an afternoon. Retries, signatures, monitoring — it just works.
socialProof.quote2: The observability alone is worth it. We can see every delivery attempt, debug failures in seconds, and replay with one click.
socialProof.quote3: Our team was spending 20% of sprint capacity maintaining webhook infrastructure. Now it's zero.

problem.title: Building webhooks in-house
problem.subtitle: Most teams underestimate the complexity. What starts as a simple HTTP POST becomes months of engineering debt.
problem.item1Before: Build custom retry logic from scratch
problem.item1After: Automatic exponential backoff, configurable per endpoint
problem.item2After: Full attempt history with HTTP details
problem.item4Before: No replay capability when things break
problem.item5Before: Custom code to receive third-party webhooks
problem.item5After: Managed ingress URLs with HMAC validation per provider
problem.item6Before: No unified view of incoming and outgoing events
problem.item6After: Single dashboard for both directions, DLQ + monitoring built in
problem.item7Before: Provider changes payload schema silently, your integration breaks
problem.item7After: Schema registry detects breaking changes and blocks invalid payloads automatically
problem.saveDesc: Stop reinventing webhook infrastructure. Ship features instead.

integrations.title: Works with your
integrations.subtitle: Official SDKs, OpenAPI spec, bidirectional webhook support, and delivery to any HTTP endpoint. Integrate in minutes, not days.
integrations.sdksDesc: Type-safe clients with built-in signature verification
integrations.restDesc: Full OpenAPI 3.0 spec. Generate clients in any language.
integrations.deliverToDesc: Deliver to any endpoint. Receive from any provider. No vendor lock-in.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.q1: How does Hookflow ensure reliable delivery?
faq.a1: Every event is persisted using a transactional outbox pattern — the event and outbox entry are written in a single database transaction. A publisher polls the outbox and sends events to Kafka. Workers consume from Kafka and deliver with automatic retries using exponential backoff. Zero events are lost, ever.
faq.q2: What happens when an endpoint is down?
faq.a2: Hookflow automatically retries with exponential backoff across multiple intervals. Failed deliveries land in the Dead Letter Queue where you can inspect, debug, and replay them with one click. You get full visibility into every attempt.
faq.q3: How do I verify webhook signatures?
faq.a3: Every delivery includes an X-Signature header with an HMAC-SHA256 hash. Each endpoint gets a unique signing secret. Our SDKs include built-in verification helpers, or you can compute the hash yourself with any HMAC library.
faq.a4: Yes. Hookflow is fully open-source and ships as Docker containers. Run it on your own infrastructure with Docker Compose, Kubernetes, or any container orchestrator. You own your data completely.
faq.q5: What's included in the free tier?
faq.a5: The free tier includes unlimited projects, endpoints, and events. You get full access to all features: retries, HMAC signatures, analytics, DLQ, audit logs, and team management. No credit card required.
faq.q6: How long does it take to integrate?
faq.a6: Most teams are up and running in under 5 minutes. Create a project, register an endpoint, and send your first event with a single API call. Our quickstart guide walks you through every step.
faq.q7: Can Hookflow receive incoming webhooks from third-party services?
faq.a7: Yes. Create an incoming source for any provider (Stripe, GitHub, Twilio, etc.), and Hookflow generates a unique ingress URL. Incoming payloads are validated with HMAC, stored, and forwarded to your internal destinations — with retries and full observability.
faq.q8: What's the difference between outgoing and incoming webhooks?
faq.a8: Outgoing: your app sends events → Hookflow delivers to your customers' endpoints. Incoming: third-party services send webhooks → Hookflow ingests, validates, and routes them to your internal APIs. Both directions share the same retry, signature, and observability features.
faq.q9: What is the Schema Registry and how does it work?
faq.a9: The Schema Registry lets you define JSON Schema contracts for each event type. Every new version is automatically diffed against the previous one, breaking changes are flagged, and you can enforce WARN or BLOCK policies. Think Kafka Schema Registry, but for HTTP webhooks.
faq.q10: How does wildcard event routing work?
faq.a10: Subscriptions support glob-style patterns: order.* matches single-segment (order.created, order.updated), order.** matches multi-segment (order.line.added), and ** catches everything. Pattern validation is enforced at creation time.

quickstartPage.title: Try Hookflow — no signup needed
quickstartPage.subtitle: Walk through the entire flow: create a project, set up endpoints, send webhooks, and monitor deliveries — all in an interactive simulation.

step1.title: Create a project
step1.desc: A project organizes your endpoints, subscriptions, API keys, and events.

step2.title: Generate an API key
step2.desc: API keys authenticate your webhook events. Each project can have multiple keys.
step2.warning: This key is shown only once. Copy it now and store it securely — you'll need it to send events.

step3.title: Create a webhook endpoint
step3.desc: Register the URL where you want to receive webhooks. Each endpoint gets a unique signing secret.
step3.secretHint: Save this secret — you'll need it to verify webhook signatures on your server.

step4.title: Subscribe to event types
step4.desc: Choose which events this endpoint should receive. Only matching events will be delivered.

step5.title: Send your first event
step5.desc: Fire a real webhook and watch it flow through the delivery pipeline — signed, delivered, confirmed.

step6.title: Monitor your deliveries
step6.desc: Every delivery is tracked with full attempt history, latency, and response codes.

feat.realtimeDesc: See every delivery as it happens with live status updates.
feat.replayDesc: Replay any failed delivery — same payload, same endpoint, no code changes.

cta.title: Ready to try it for real?
cta.subtitle: Create a free account and send your first webhook in under 5 minutes.

overview.title: API Documentation
overview.subtitle: Complete reference for integrating bidirectional webhook infrastructure into your application.
overview.whatIsDesc1: Hookflow is a bidirectional webhook infrastructure that handles outgoing delivery and incoming ingestion with retries, signatures, and monitoring.
overview.whatIsDesc2: Send events to your customers' endpoints (outgoing), or receive webhooks from third-party providers like Stripe and GitHub (incoming). Both directions share the same reliability and observability features.

concepts.eventDesc: A data payload you send to our API that needs to be delivered to webhook endpoints.
concepts.endpointDesc: A URL where webhooks are delivered. Each endpoint has a unique secret for signature verification.
concepts.subscriptionDesc: Links an endpoint to specific event types, controlling which events get delivered where.
concepts.deliveryDesc: A single webhook delivery attempt to an endpoint, with full attempt history and status.
concepts.attemptDesc: Each retry of a delivery, including HTTP status, error message, and latency.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.subtitle: Two authentication methods: JWT for dashboard access and API keys for event ingestion.
auth.jwtDesc: Use JWT tokens to access dashboard endpoints like projects, endpoints, and deliveries.
auth.usingJwtDesc: Include the access token in the Authorization header for all authenticated requests:
auth.apiKeyDesc: Use API keys to send events to the platform. Each project has its own API keys.
auth.securityNoteDesc: Never expose API keys in client-side code or public repositories. Store them securely in environment variables.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.subtitle: Complete workflow from registration to sending your first webhook.
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Create an account and get your JWT token.
gettingStarted.step2Desc: Projects organize your webhooks, endpoints, and events.
gettingStarted.step3Desc: Generate an API key to send events.
gettingStarted.step4Desc: Add a URL where you want to receive webhooks.
gettingStarted.step5Desc: Subscribe the endpoint to specific event types.
gettingStarted.step6Desc: Send your first event using the API key.

eventsApi.title: Events API
eventsApi.subtitle: Send events that will be delivered to subscribed webhook endpoints.
eventsApi.sendEventDesc: Send an event to be delivered to all subscribed endpoints. Events are processed asynchronously and queued for delivery.
eventsApi.idempotencyDesc: Use the Idempotency-Key header to safely retry requests. If the same key is sent within 24 hours, the original response will be returned without creating duplicate events.

endpointsApi.title: Endpoints API
endpointsApi.subtitle: Manage webhook endpoints where events are delivered.
endpointsApi.createDesc: Add a new webhook endpoint to receive events.
endpointsApi.listDesc: Get all endpoints for a project.
endpointsApi.deleteDesc: Deletes an endpoint and all its subscriptions.
endpointsApi.rotateSecretDesc: Generate a new webhook secret for an endpoint.
endpointsApi.testDesc: Sends a test webhook to verify endpoint connectivity.

subscriptionsApi.title: Subscriptions API
subscriptionsApi.subtitle: Route specific event types to endpoints.
subscriptionsApi.createDesc: Subscribe an endpoint to event types.
subscriptionsApi.listDesc: Get all subscriptions for a project.

deliveriesApi.title: Deliveries API
deliveriesApi.subtitle: Monitor and manage webhook deliveries.
deliveriesApi.listDesc: Get all deliveries for a project with filters.
deliveriesApi.getAttemptsDesc: View all retry attempts for a delivery.
deliveriesApi.bulkReplayDesc: Re-send multiple failed deliveries at once. Filter by status, endpoint, or provide specific delivery IDs.

security.title: Webhook Security
security.subtitle: Verify webhook authenticity using HMAC signatures.
security.sigVerificationDesc: Every webhook includes an X-Signature header with an HMAC-SHA256 signature.
security.endpointVerificationDesc: When you register an endpoint, we send a verification challenge to confirm you own the URL.
security.challengeRequestDesc: We POST a JSON payload with type webhook.verification:
security.expectedResponseDesc: Return the challenge value in your response:

errors.title: Errors & Rate Limits
errors.subtitle: HTTP status codes, error responses, and rate limiting.
errors.rateLimitingDesc: The Events API is rate limited to protect the platform. Rate limit information is included in response headers.
errors.rateLimitExceededDesc: When rate limited, wait until X-RateLimit-Reset timestamp before retrying.
errors.validationErrorsDesc: When request validation fails, you'll receive detailed field-level errors.

sdks.title: SDKs
sdks.subtitle: Official client libraries for Node.js, Python, and PHP — with full TypeScript / type-hint support.

incomingWebhooks.title: Incoming Webhooks
incomingWebhooks.subtitle: Receive, validate, and forward webhooks from third-party providers to your internal APIs.
incomingWebhooks.overviewDesc1: Incoming webhooks let you receive events from external services (Stripe, GitHub, Twilio, etc.) through managed ingress URLs. Each incoming source gets a unique URL, optional HMAC verification, and automatic forwarding to your internal destinations.
incomingWebhooks.overviewDesc2: The incoming flow: Provider sends POST → Hookflow ingress URL → HMAC validation → stored as incoming event → forwarded to configured destinations with retries.
incomingWebhooks.createSourceDesc: Create a new incoming source with a unique ingress URL. Configure the provider type and optional HMAC verification.
incomingWebhooks.listSourcesDesc: Get all incoming sources for a project.
incomingWebhooks.getSourceDesc: Returns source details including the ingress URL.
incomingWebhooks.deleteSourceDesc: Deletes a source and all its destinations.
incomingWebhooks.createDestDesc: Add a forwarding destination to an incoming source. Validated webhooks will be forwarded to this URL.
incomingWebhooks.listDestsDesc: Get all destinations for a source.
incomingWebhooks.listEventsDesc: Get all incoming events for a project, with optional source filtering.
incomingWebhooks.getEventDesc: Returns full event details including request headers, body, verification status, and forwarding results.
incomingWebhooks.replayEventDesc: Re-forward an incoming event to all configured destinations.
incomingWebhooks.ingressUrlDesc: Each source gets a unique, opaque ingress URL when created:
incomingWebhooks.verificationNone: NONE — No verification (not recommended for production)
incomingWebhooks.verificationHmac: HMAC_GENERIC — Verify using provider's HMAC signature
incomingWebhooks.providerTypes: Supported provider types: GENERIC, STRIPE, GITHUB, TWILIO, SHOPIFY, HUBSPOT, SLACK, CUSTOM

schemaRegistry.title: Schema Registry
schemaRegistry.subtitle: Define JSON Schema contracts, version schemas, detect breaking changes, and enforce validation policies per project.
schemaRegistry.overviewDesc1: The Schema Registry brings contract testing to HTTP webhooks. Define a JSON Schema for each event type, upload new versions, and Hookflow automatically diffs them — flagging added fields, removed fields, type changes, and breaking changes.
schemaRegistry.overviewDesc2: Enable schema validation per project with WARN (log mismatches) or BLOCK (reject invalid payloads) policies. Think Kafka Schema Registry, but for webhooks.
schemaRegistry.createEventTypeDesc: Register a new event type in the project catalog. Names must be lowercase with dots/underscores (e.g. order.created).
schemaRegistry.listEventTypesDesc: Returns all event types for the project, including latest version number, active status, and breaking change flag.
schemaRegistry.updateEventTypeDesc: Update the description of an existing event type.
schemaRegistry.deleteEventTypeDesc: Delete an event type and all its schema versions.
schemaRegistry.createVersionDesc: Upload a new JSON Schema version for an event type. Created as DRAFT. Duplicate schemas are deduplicated by fingerprint.
schemaRegistry.listVersionsDesc: Returns all schema versions for an event type, ordered by version number descending.
schemaRegistry.promoteVersionDesc: Promotes a DRAFT schema to ACTIVE. The previous ACTIVE version is automatically deprecated.
schemaRegistry.deprecateVersionDesc: Sets a schema version status to DEPRECATED.
schemaRegistry.listChangesDesc: Returns the diff history for a specific event type — added/removed fields, type changes, and breaking change flags.
schemaRegistry.listProjectChangesDesc: Returns the global diff feed across all event types in the project, with event type names.
schemaRegistry.validationPoliciesDesc: Enable schema validation on a project to automatically validate every incoming event payload against the active schema:
schemaRegistry.policyWarn: WARN — Log validation errors but accept the event
schemaRegistry.policyBlock: BLOCK — Reject the event with 400 if payload doesn't match the active schema
schemaRegistry.wildcardRoutingDesc: Subscriptions support glob-style patterns for event type matching:
schemaRegistry.compatibilityModesDesc: Each schema version has a compatibility mode that controls how changes are evaluated:
schemaRegistry.compatBackward: BACKWARD — New schema can read data written by the old schema
schemaRegistry.compatForward: FORWARD — Old schema can read data written by the new schema
schemaRegistry.compatFull: FULL — Both backward and forward compatible

deterministicReplay.title: Deterministic Replay
deterministicReplay.subtitle: Preview replays before sending, replay from any attempt step, and leverage Idempotency-Key headers for safe deduplication.
deterministicReplay.overviewDesc: Deterministic Replay gives you full control over webhook re-delivery. Preview what will happen before replaying, choose exactly which attempt to resume from, and ensure every outgoing request carries an Idempotency-Key header so your consumers can safely deduplicate.
deterministicReplay.idempotencyKeyHeaderDesc: Every outgoing webhook includes an Idempotency-Key header derived from the event's idempotency key and the target endpoint ID. If the event was ingested with an explicit key, it is reused; under AUTO policy a key is generated from the event ID. Consumers can use this header to detect and discard duplicate deliveries.
deterministicReplay.dryRunDesc: Call the replay endpoint with ?dryRun=true to get a full preview without actually sending. The response includes the target URL, idempotency key, event type, payload summary, previous attempts, and a human-readable plan string.
deterministicReplay.replayFromAttemptDesc: Pass ?fromAttempt=N to resume delivery from attempt N instead of restarting from scratch. The delivery's attempt counter is set to N and it is re-queued for immediate dispatch.
deterministicReplay.replayFromAttemptNote: Useful when an early attempt partially succeeded and you want to skip retrying from the beginning.
deterministicReplay.idempotencyPoliciesDesc: Configure per-project idempotency behaviour in Settings → Schemas & Policies:
deterministicReplay.policyNone: No idempotency key handling. Keys are optional and not auto-generated.
deterministicReplay.policyAuto: Auto-generate an idempotency key from the event ID if the client does not provide one.
deterministicReplay.policyRequired: Reject events that do not include an Idempotency-Key header with 400 Bad Request.

incomingSources.title: Incoming Sources
incomingSources.subtitle: Manage incoming webhook sources for {{project}}
incomingSources.noSourcesDesc: Create your first incoming source to start receiving webhooks from external providers
incomingSources.name: Name
incomingSources.rateLimitHint: Requests per second (leave empty for unlimited)

createDialog.title: Create Incoming Source
createDialog.description: Configure a new incoming webhook source to receive events from external providers
createDialog.name: Source Name
createDialog.slugHint: URL-friendly identifier. Auto-generated from name if empty.
createDialog.hmacSecretHint: The secret used by the provider to sign webhook payloads. Stored encrypted.

editDialog.title: Edit Incoming Source
editDialog.description: Update the incoming webhook source configuration

deleteDialog.title: Disable incoming source?
deleteDialog.description: This source will be disabled and stop accepting incoming webhooks. Existing events are preserved.

security.noVerificationDesc: Without HMAC verification, anyone with the ingress URL can send events. Consider enabling HMAC_GENERIC or PROVIDER verification for production use.
security.rotateHmacHint: Rotate your HMAC secret periodically to maintain security. Update the secret here and in your provider's webhook settings simultaneously.

howToSend.title: How to send webhooks
howToSend.description: Configure your provider to send webhooks to the ingress URL below:

incomingDestinations.title: Forwarding Destinations
incomingDestinations.subtitle: Where incoming webhooks are forwarded to
incomingDestinations.noDestinationsDesc: Add a forwarding destination to route incoming webhooks to your application

createDialog.title: Add Forwarding Destination
createDialog.description: Configure where incoming webhooks should be forwarded
createDialog.payloadTransformHint: Optional JSONPath expression to transform payload before forwarding
createDialog.transformationHint: Select a reusable transformation or use the inline JSONPath field below.
createDialog.transformationOverrides: Saved transformation takes priority over the inline JSONPath field below.

validation.headersInvalidJson: Custom headers must be valid JSON
validation.transformInvalidJson: Payload transform must be valid JSON
validation.attemptsRange: Max attempts must be between 1 and 20
validation.timeoutRange: Timeout must be between 1 and 120 seconds
validation.retryDelaysFormat: Retry delays must be comma-separated positive numbers (seconds)

deleteDialog.title: Delete destination?
deleteDialog.description: This destination will be permanently deleted. Incoming webhooks will no longer be forwarded to this URL.

incomingEvents.title: Incoming Events
incomingEvents.subtitle: Incoming webhook events for {{project}}
incomingEvents.noEventsDesc: Events will appear here when external providers send webhooks to your sources
incomingEvents.noEventsNoSourcesDesc: You need to create an incoming source first. Set up an ingress endpoint, then send webhooks to it.

replay.title: Replay Event?
replay.description: This will re-send the event to all enabled destinations. Note: this may cause duplicate processing if the destination is not idempotent.
replay.idempotencyWarning: Ensure your destinations handle duplicate events (idempotency).

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

accessDenied.title: Access Denied
accessDenied.description: You don't have permission to view this page. Contact your organization owner to request access.

verifyEmail.successDesc: Your email has been successfully verified. You can now use all features.
verifyEmail.failed: Verification failed. The token may be invalid or expired.

deliveryDetails.title: Delivery Details
deliveryDetails.description: View delivery status, attempts, and responses
deliveryDetails.noAttempts: No attempts recorded yet for this delivery.

replayDialog.title: Replay Delivery?
replayDialog.description: This will re-attempt delivery of the webhook to the endpoint. The delivery will be queued for immediate processing.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting Tips
troubleshooting.tip1: Verify the endpoint URL is accessible and responding
troubleshooting.tip2: Check webhook signature validation in your application
troubleshooting.tip3: Review rate limits and ensure they're not exceeded
troubleshooting.tip4: Inspect the error messages in delivery attempts below
troubleshooting.tip5: Consider replaying the delivery after fixing the issue

diagnosis.title: Error Diagnosis
diagnosis.noMoreRetries: No more retries — moved to DLQ

timeout.label: Connection Timeout
timeout.fix: Your endpoint took too long to respond. Increase the timeout on your server or optimize the webhook handler to respond within 30 seconds.

connRefused.label: Connection Refused
connRefused.fix: The endpoint server is not accepting connections. Verify the server is running, the port is correct, and firewall rules allow inbound traffic.

dns.label: DNS Resolution Failed
dns.fix: The endpoint hostname could not be resolved. Check the URL for typos and verify DNS records are configured correctly.

ssl.label: SSL/TLS Error
ssl.fix: The SSL handshake failed. Ensure the SSL certificate is valid, not expired, and the certificate chain is complete.

auth.label: Authentication Rejected
auth.fix: The endpoint returned 401/403. Verify the webhook signature validation logic on your server and check that secrets match.

notFound.label: Endpoint Not Found
notFound.fix: The endpoint returned 404. Verify the webhook URL path is correct and the route is registered on your server.

payloadTooLarge.label: Payload Too Large
payloadTooLarge.fix: The endpoint returned 413. Increase the request body size limit on your server or reduce the event payload size.

rateLimited.label: Rate Limited
rateLimited.fix: The endpoint returned 429. Your server is rejecting requests due to rate limiting. The delivery will be retried automatically with backoff.

clientError.label: Client Error (4xx)
clientError.fix: The endpoint rejected the request. Check the response body for details and verify your webhook handler accepts the expected content type and format.

serverError.label: Server Error (5xx)
serverError.fix: The endpoint server encountered an internal error. Check your server logs for exceptions. The delivery will be retried automatically.

unknown.label: Unknown Error
unknown.fix: An unexpected error occurred. Check the error message and response body for details.

piiRules.title: PII Masking Rules
piiRules.subtitle: Configure which fields are masked before sharing event payloads
piiRules.newRuleDesc: Specify a JSON path or pattern name to mask in event payloads
piiRules.noRulesHint: Seed defaults to add email, phone, and card masking

deleteDialog.title: Delete masking rule?
deleteDialog.description: This rule will be permanently removed. Event payloads will no longer be masked by this rule.

sharedDebug.title: Shared Debug Link
sharedDebug.subtitle: PII-masked event payload shared for debugging
sharedDebug.expired: This debug link has expired or does not exist.
sharedDebug.error: Something went wrong loading this debug link.
sharedDebug.footer: Sensitive data has been masked. This link will expire automatically.

eventDiff.title: Event Diff
eventDiff.subtitle: Compare two event payloads side by side
eventDiff.noDiffs: No differences found — payloads are identical

schemas.title: Schema Registry
schemas.subtitle: Manage event type contracts, versioned schemas, and track breaking changes
schemas.eventTypeNameHint: Use dot notation, e.g. order.created, user.updated
schemas.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional description of this event type
schemas.noSearchResults: No event types match your search
schemas.noEventTypesHint: Create one manually or send an event — schemas are auto-discovered
schemas.confirmDelete: Delete event type "{{name}}" and all its schema versions?
schemas.uploadHint: Schema will be fingerprinted and diffed automatically
schemas.invalidJson: Invalid JSON — please check your schema
schemas.noVersionsHint: Upload a JSON schema or send an event to auto-discover one
schemas.noChangesHint: Changes appear when new schema versions are uploaded
schemas.selectEventTypeHint: Choose an event type from the left panel to view its schemas and history

validation.title: Schema Validation
validation.description: Validate incoming events against active schemas
validation.enabledHint: When enabled, incoming events are validated against the active schema for their event type
validation.policyWarn: Warn — log validation errors but accept the event
validation.policyBlock: Block — reject events that fail schema validation

idempotency.title: Idempotency Policy
idempotency.hint: Control how idempotency keys are handled for event ingestion and outgoing webhooks

onboarding.title: Getting Started
onboarding.subtitle: Complete these steps to set up your webhook infrastructure

steps.createProjectDesc: Projects organize your endpoints, events, and API keys
steps.createEndpointDesc: Register the URL where you want to receive webhooks
steps.createSubscriptionDesc: Link your endpoint to specific event types
steps.createApiKeyDesc: You'll need an API key to send events programmatically
steps.sendEventDesc: Send a test event and see it delivered to your endpoint
steps.checkDeliveryDesc: Check the delivery log to confirm your webhook was received
steps.createIncomingSourceDesc: Set up an ingress endpoint to receive webhooks from GitHub, Stripe, or any provider
steps.enableHmacDesc: Secure your ingress by enabling signature verification — prevents unauthorized event injection
steps.createIncomingDestinationDesc: Configure the URL where incoming events should be forwarded to
steps.testIncomingCurlDesc: Use the ingress URL to send a cURL request and verify the source accepts events
steps.verifyForwardingDesc: Check the incoming events page to confirm events are received and forwarded

onboarding.allDone: You're all set! Your webhook infrastructure is ready.

welcome.title: Welcome to Hookflow! 🎉
welcome.description: We'll walk you through the key steps to set up your webhook infrastructure in just a few minutes.

project.title: Create a Project
project.description: Projects are the top-level containers that organize your endpoints, events, subscriptions, and API keys.
project.tip: Think of projects as separate environments — e.g. "Production", "Staging", or per-customer.

endpoint.title: Register an Endpoint
endpoint.description: Endpoints are the URLs where your webhooks will be delivered. Add the URL of your server that will receive webhook events.
endpoint.tip: Each endpoint gets a unique signing secret for HMAC signature verification.

subscription.title: Subscribe to Events
subscription.description: Subscriptions link your endpoints to specific event types. Only events matching the subscription will be delivered.
subscription.tip: Use wildcards like "order.*" to subscribe to all order-related events.

apiKey.title: Generate an API Key
apiKey.description: API keys are used to authenticate when sending events programmatically from your backend services.
apiKey.tip: Store your API key securely — it's shown only once at creation time.

send.title: Send Your First Event
send.description: Use the API or the UI to send a test event. It will be matched against subscriptions and delivered to the right endpoints.
send.tip: Check the Deliveries page to see real-time status, attempts, and response details.

done.title: You're All Set!
done.description: Your webhook infrastructure is ready. Head to your project to start sending events and monitoring deliveries.

footer.tagline: Production-ready webhook infrastructure. Reliable delivery at any scale.

invite.successDesc: You are now a member of the organization. Welcome aboard!
invite.noToken: Invalid invite link. Token or organization ID is missing.
invite.failed: Failed to accept invitation. The token may be invalid or expired.
invite.loginRequiredDesc: You need to sign in to accept this invitation.
invite.newUserHint: Don't have an account yet? Register first, then you'll be redirected back to accept the invite.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Monitor delivery health and get notified of issues
alerts.noEventsDesc: Alert rules will fire events here when conditions are met
alerts.noRulesDesc: Create your first alert rule to monitor delivery health

deleteDialog.title: Delete alert rule?
deleteDialog.description: This will permanently delete the rule and stop monitoring. Existing alert events will remain.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.emptyDesc: Incidents group related failures for tracking and RCA
incidents.rcaPlaceholder: Document the root cause, impact, and remediation steps...

createDialog.title: Create Incident
createDialog.desc: Track a group of related failures

usage.title: Usage & Metrics
usage.subtitle: Resource usage for {{project}}
usage.noDataDesc: Start sending events to see usage metrics

transformations.title: Transformations
transformations.subtitle: Reusable payload transformation templates for normalizing and reshaping webhook data.
transformations.createDesc: Define a reusable JSON template with ${$.jsonpath} expressions to reshape payloads.
transformations.editDesc: Update the transformation template. Changing the template auto-increments the version.
transformations.emptyDesc: Create reusable transformations to normalize payloads across subscriptions and incoming destinations.
transformations.name: Name

deleteDialog.title: Delete Transformation
deleteDialog.description: This will remove the transformation. Subscriptions and destinations referencing it will lose their transformation link. This action cannot be undone.
